service: Expanded Centenarian Program Payout (Ages 80, 85, 90, 95)
description: Provision of cash incentives for elderly residents reaching milestone ages of 80, 85, 90, and 95, facilitated by the National Commission of Senior Citizens (NCSC).
steps: Verify if the senior's name is included in the official NCSC Masterlist (January to June 2025 batch).
Secure required templates (Authorization, SPA, or Warranty & Release forms) from the OSCA Office between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM.
Prepare the specific set of documents based on the claimant's status (Personal, Representative, or Deceased).
Proceed to the Municipal Treasury Office on the designated payout date and time.
Submit documents for verification and receive the cash incentive.
requirements: FOR PERSONAL CLAIM: 2 copies of valid ID with signature or thumbmark.
FOR REPRESENTATIVE: Authorization Letter (NCSC template), Physical ID of grantee (original + photocopy), Physical ID of claimant (original + photocopy).
FOR REPRESENTATIVE (BEDRIDDEN): Barangay Certification signed by Captain and BHW or Medical Certificate.
FOR REPRESENTATIVE (PROOF OF LIFE): Half-body photo of the grantee holding a current calendar or newspaper.

FOR DECEASED GRANTEE: Special Power of Attorney (SPA template), Warranty and Release from Liability form, Original or CTC of Death Certificate, Claimant ID with 3 signatures, and Proof of Relationship.

faqs.question: Where can I get the SPA or Authorization templates?
faqs.answer: Templates are available at the Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs (OSCA) from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this specific payout?
faqs.answer: Seniors who are part of the qualified beneficiaries list from January to June 2025 provided by the NCSC.

quickInfo.documents: Valid IDs, SPA/Authorization, Proof of Life

sources.name: Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs (OSCA) Los Baños

service: Fire Safety Inspection Certificate (FSIC)

service: Garbage Collection Schedule
description: Weekly schedule for waste collection per barangay. Please segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste.
steps: Monday & Thursday: Batong Malake, San Antonio, Maahas (Biodegradable)
Tuesday & Friday: Bayog, Mayondon, Malinta (Biodegradable)
Wednesday: All Barangays (Non-Biodegradable / Recyclables)
Saturday: Special collection for Market area
Sunday: No Collection
requirements: Please ensure waste is segregated before putting it out.
Bring out trash only between 6:00 AM - 8:00 AM.

sources.name: Republic Act No. 9003 (Ecological Solid Waste Management Act)

Construction Safety and Health Program (CSHP - DOLE)

service: Issuance of Tax Declaration for Transfer of Ownership Consolidation/Subdivision
whoMayAvail: Real Property Owners and other agencies

Clearances from BIR and RD

clientSteps.action: Present and Submit all the requirements

sub_steps.action: Inspects and verifies documents for completeness and validity; Verification from previous record/tax declaration

clientSteps.action: Receive payment order form and Pay at the cashier

sub_steps.action: Issue payment order form and advises applicant to Pay the required fee at the cashier
Pay Annotation Fee per Tax Declaration (₱100.00)

clientSteps.action: Receive Transaction Claim Stub with Date of New Tax Declaration Release

sub_steps.action: Issue Claim stub to client and explains the variability of new TD release

clientSteps.action: Field inspection and Property Assessment

sub_steps.action: Shall be Present, OR send a representative, to accompany the office's Field Personnel during the inspection of the property being transacted most especially if the property has an improvement
Corrects Property Boundaries, assigns new PIN, Prepare Field Work map
Conducts inspection of Property being Transacted
Prepare inspection Report

clientSteps.action: Records the Transaction in the TMCR (Tax Mapping Control Roll)

sub_steps.action: Records the Transaction in the TMCR (Tax Mapping Control Roll)
Prepare Initial FAAS based on inspection Report
Review and preapproves Initial FAAS
Assigns new TD No. and records in the Logbook of Assessment Records
Prepare New Tax Declaration / Notice of Assessment and Attach all pertinent documents
Sign Final FAAS and New TD

clientSteps.action: Upon date of return, Receive approved Tax Declaration

sub_steps.action: Issue Owner's copy & Notice of Assessment, and have the claimant Sign Office Duplicate and Logbook

turnaroundTime: 21-35 working days (21 days if no improvement, 35 days if with improvement)
plainLanguageName: Get a Tax Declaration for Transfer of Ownership Consolidation/Subdivision

service: Issuance of Updated Tax Declaration for Corrected Information e.g. Spelling of Name, Lot Area, Lot Number, etc...
whoMayAvail: Real Property Owners and other agencies

detailedRequirements.requirement: Application Form
Request Letter indicating the information that needs correction / update
Latest Realty Tax Receipt / Tax Clearance
Previous Tax Declaration
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Owner as received from the Assessor's Office

detailedRequirements.requirement: Annotation Fee
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Issued at Transaction window, paid at Municipal Treasurer's Office

clientSteps.action: Present/Submit all the requirements
Pay annotation fee and Submit receipt to Assessor's Office; Receive Transaction Claim Stub

If Transaction falls in the SIMPLE category: 7 working days wait time. If property falls in the COMPLEX category: Early on the 21 or 35 working days wait, be Present (or send representative) to accompany the office's Field Personnel during the inspection of the property being transacted
Upon date of return, Receive approved Tax Declaration, Owner's copy & Notice of Assessment, and Sign Office Duplicate and Logbook

turnaroundTime: Variable by transaction type: **simple** (real properties with recent assessment not older than 1 year, no field inspection required): **7 working days** **complex** (real properties with assessments older than 1 year, requiring field inspection): - properties without improvements: **21 working days** - properties with improvements: **35 working days** note: date of release can be variable due to: • volume of transactions • simple transactions: no field inspection, approval at local assessor's level only • complex transactions: requires field inspection with longer timeline • if there is an improvement in the property being transacted, reassessment may require approval of the provincial assessor
plainLanguageName: Get an Updated Tax Declaration for Corrected Information e.g. Spelling of Name, Lot Area, Lot Number, etc...

service: Issuance of Updated Tax Declaration for New or Reassessment of Building/Machineries
whoMayAvail: Real Property Owners and other agencies

detailedRequirements.requirement: Application Form
Approved Building Permit
Building Plan
Certificate of Completion
Certificate of Occupancy
Previous Tax Declaration (for reassessment)
Tax Clearance or Latest Tax Receipt (for reassessment)
Completely filled-up Sworn Statement

clientSteps.action: Present and Submit all the requirements (Application Form, Approved Building Permit, Building Plan, Certificate of Completion, Certificate of Occupancy, Previous Tax Declaration, Tax Clearance, Sworn Statement)
Receive Transaction Claim Stub
Wait for release (35 working days) - be Present or send a representative to accompany Field Personnel during property inspection
Upon date of return, Receive approved Tax Declaration and Sign Office Duplicate and Logbook

turnaroundTime: 35 working days (subject to provincial assessor approval for new assessment)
plainLanguageName: Get an Updated Tax Declaration for New or Reassessment of Building/Machineries

service: Issuance of Updated Tax Declaration for New or Reassessment of Lands
whoMayAvail: Real Property Owners and other agencies

detailedRequirements.requirement: Application Form
Annotation Fee
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Issued at Transaction window, paid at Municipal Treasurer's Office

clientSteps.action: Present/Submit all the requirements
Pay annotation fee and Submit receipt to Assessor's Office; Receive Transaction Claim Stub
Early on the 21-35 working days wait, be Present (or send representative) to accompany office's Field Personnel during inspection of the property
Upon date of return, Receive approved Tax Declaration, Owner's copy & Notice of Assessment, and Sign Office Duplicate and Logbook

turnaroundTime: Variable: 21 working days (reassessment of lands w/o improvement) | 35 working days (reassessment of lands w/ improvement or new assessment of lands w/ or w/o improvement) note: date of release can be variable due to: • volume of transactions • reassessment of lands would most likely entail change in market value and assessed value and is subject to the final approval of the provincial assessor • lands declared for the 1st time whether with or without improvements will always be subject to the final approval of the provincial assessor.
plainLanguageName: Get an Updated Tax Declaration for New or Reassessment of Lands
